evidence.scientific_clarity: Scatter + OLS line + R^2 annotation + highlighted OursModel point all present; axes use log10 labels matching Chinchilla framing; title explicit.
evidence.typography: No pdf.fonttype=42 or svg.fonttype set; pdffonts confirms all three fonts are Type 3 (not selectable/editable); default DejaVu Sans used throughout.
evidence.palette: #4878CF blue for data and #E24A33 red-orange for OursModel are distinguishable but not from an explicit CB-safe palette (e.g. Okabe-Ito); no green/red directional misuse.
evidence.layout_density: 5.5x4.2in figure with tight_layout(), top/right spines removed, annotation box top-right, OursModel label offset with arrow — no crowding or wasted space.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption matches figure numerics; legend present; 'lies on the scaling frontier' is an unsupported qualitative claim; Type 3 fonts would be flagged by journal submission system.
evidence.vector_fidelity: figure.pdf exists (27 KB) but pdffonts shows all fonts as Type 3 — text is not selectable; script never sets rcParams['pdf.fonttype']=42.
evidence.file_format: figure.pdf (27 KB), figure.png (57 KB), caption.tex (474 B), gen_figure.py (2.1 KB) all present and non-trivial; bonus figure.svg also generated.
evidence.caption_quality: Caption states n=80, gives full OLS equation (y=-0.0449x+0.621), R^2=0.877, and OursModel coordinates (10^9.45, 0.210) — specific numerical finding clearly stated.

overall_strengths: All required output files are present and non-trivial; the script is clean and reproducible.
Caption is quantitatively rich with OLS equation, R^2, and OursModel coordinates matching the in-figure annotation.
overall_weaknesses: pdf.fonttype=42 is never set, so the PDF contains Type 3 bitmapped fonts — text is not selectable or editable, which most journals reject.
Default DejaVu Sans font family used with no explicit colorblind-safe palette; 'scaling frontier' claim in caption is qualitative and unsupported.
